title: message_query: has_mention=Some(false) bypasses guard but sends unsupported filter
description: The validation guard (chat_id.is_none() && has_mention.is_none()) only blocks the case where both are None. Calling message_query with chat_id=None and has_mention=Some(false) passes the guard and sends {"hasMention": false} in the filter. Per spec, servers MUST return unsupportedFilter when neither a chatId nor hasMention:true is present. The guard should additionally check that if chat_id is None then has_mention must be Some(true); passing Some(false) is semantically equivalent to providing no filter anchor. The error message also misleads: 'at least one of chat_id or has_mention must be provided' implies Some(false) is acceptable.
